input.command:
git add scripts/dev.ts README.md && git status && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(dev): bun-based one-shot runner with proper concurrent sequencing

scripts/dev.{sh,ps1} relied on `firebase emulators:exec`, which nests
the child command inside the emulator process tree. That works but is
opaque: signal handling is one big tree, port-readiness is implicit,
and the OS-specific quoting was fragile.

Replaced with scripts/dev.ts (Bun, cross-platform). It:

- Spawns `firebase emulators:start` as its own child
- Polls Firestore (8080) + Auth (9099) + Functions (5001) with a short
  TCP probe loop until all three accept connections — explicit
  port-readiness instead of "wait some seconds and hope"
- Runs `npm run seed` only when ./.emulator-data/ doesn't exist yet,
  with the correct FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST + FIREBASE_AUTH_EMULATOR_HOST
  + GCLOUD_PROJECT envs injected
- Spawns `flutter run` concurrently against the live emulator
- Forwards SIGINT/SIGTERM to the emulator child so --export-on-exit
  fires cleanly on Ctrl-C and the .emulator-data dir gets written

bun is a single binary; install one-time per machine via the standard
bun.sh installer or `npm install -g bun`. The existing shell-script
runners stay for fallback.

README "Daily run loop" now leads with the Bun command and points the
shell scripts as the no-Bun fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <[REDACTED:email]>
EOF
)" && git push

content.text:
Pushed `c42aae9`. New runner:

```bash
bun scripts/dev.ts
```

Behavior:
1. Spawns `firebase emulators:start --only auth,firestore,functions,ui` as its own child
2. TCP-probes ports 8080 / 9099 / 5001 every 500ms until all three answer (explicit ready signal, no sleep-and-hope)
3. If `.emulator-data/` absent → runs seed once with all 3 env vars
4. Spawns `flutter run` concurrently — both children live in parallel under this script
5. SIGINT forwarded to emulator so `--export-on-exit` triggers cleanup + state save

Install Bun once (mise shim is set up but unset — pin it):

```bash
mise use -g bun@latest
# or
npm install -g bun
```

Then:

```bash
# stop any current emulator first (q in flutter, or pkill -f emulators)
git pull
rm -rf .emulator-data    # optional — start fresh
bun scripts/dev.ts
```

Browser → http://127.0.0.1:4000 should load Emulator UI within a few seconds of "[dev] emulators up".

Shell scripts `dev.sh` / `dev.ps1` remain as fallback for anyone who doesn't want Bun.

gh pr edit 7 --title "chore: deploy removal + docs alignment + admin uid rename + bun dev runner" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
Multi-purpose cleanup PR (grew from "just remove deploy workflows" as adjacent issues surfaced during local testing). Five themed changes; each commit is self-contained.

### 1. Remove deploy workflows (`.github/workflows/`)
Class project runs on the Firebase Emulator Suite only; the two deploy workflows hard-failed on every push to \`main\` because the \`FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT\` secret is empty. Deleted both workflows. Recreate from git history later if real-Firebase deploy is needed.

### 2. Docs alignment with current reality
- README "Status" no longer claims \`functions/src/lib/*\` is stub; the lib is fully implemented.
- README "Known gaps" rewritten: removed the stub claim, pointed at ADR-0002 for the FCM scope carve-out, documented the no-live-deploy stance.
- README "How to read these docs" + \`docs/README.md\` reading order now include \`docs/adr/\`.
- \`docs/08-implementation-diagram.md\` no longer shows the deploy action edges + \`FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT\` secrets node; developer-OS labels corrected to match ADR-0001 (1 Linux, 4 Windows).
- \`docs/06-wbs.md\` WBS 8.2 + 8.3 marked deferred (0 days).
- \`docs/superpowers/specs/...md\` §15 deploy entries struck through with a pointer to ADR-0002.

### 3. Admin uid rename: \`admin-aok\` → \`droneaid-admin\`
Decouples the seeded admin persona from the lead developer's handle so the database identity is product-neutral. Touched:
- \`functions/src/seed/seedAdmins.ts\`
- \`functions/__rules_tests__/firestore.rules.test.ts\` (4 references)
- \`docs/09-page-flow-design.md\` §4.1 seed table + weather example

Also brought \`docs/09-page-flow-design.md\` §4.1 up to date with the earlier checksum fix (national ID \`[REDACTED:thai-id]\`, name \`"Drone-Aid Admin"\`).

### 4. Login demo card label
\`app/lib/features/auth/login_page.dart\` previously displayed "Aok (admin)" on the demo accounts card. Now shows the neutral label "Admin" to match the renamed persona.

### 5. One-shot dev runner (\`scripts/dev.ts\`, Bun)
Replaces the three-terminal manual flow with one command:

\`\`\`bash
bun scripts/dev.ts
\`\`\`

Behavior:
- Spawns \`firebase emulators:start --only auth,firestore,functions,ui\` in a child process
- TCP-probes ports 8080/9099/5001 every 500ms until all three accept connections
- If \`./.emulator-data/\` doesn't exist (first run): runs \`npm run seed\` with the right env vars, then exports state to \`./.emulator-data\` on Ctrl-C
- If it does exist: imports it, skips seed
- Spawns \`flutter run\` concurrently
- Forwards SIGINT to the emulator so the export-on-exit fires cleanly

Earlier shell-script runners (\`scripts/dev.sh\`, \`scripts/dev.ps1\`) were dropped in favour of the single Bun script.

\`./.emulator-data/\` added to \`.gitignore\` (machine-local state with seeded auth records).

## Test plan
- [x] \`npm run build\` in \`functions/\` clean
- [x] \`flutter analyze\` on changed Dart files clean
- [x] Manual: \`bun scripts/dev.ts\` brings up emulators, seeds on first run, launches Flutter on Android emulator, login as both \`[REDACTED:thai-id] + Admin#001\` (lands on \`/admin/requests\`) and \`[REDACTED:thai-id] + Demo#101\` (lands on \`/user/home\`)
- [x] Emulator UI reachable at \`http://127.0.0.1:4000\`
- [x] After \`q\` quit, \`./.emulator-data/\` directory written; subsequent \`bun scripts/dev.ts\` skips seed and keeps state
- [ ] CI green on this branch

content.content:
log-presence:
    name: Agent log presence
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.13'
      - name: Check that PR author committed a session log
        env:
          PR_AUTHOR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        run: |
          python - <<'PY'
          import os, re, subprocess, sys
          author = os.environ.get("PR_AUTHOR", "")
          base = os.environ.get("BASE_SHA", "")
          head = os.environ.get("HEAD_SHA", "")

          # Resolve GitHub handle → folder name via docs/agent-logs/README.md table.
          mapping = {}
          for line in open("docs/agent-logs/README.md", encoding="utf-8"):
              m = re.match(r"\|\s*\w+\s*\|\s*`?(\w+)`?\s*\|\s*@?([\w-]+)\s*\|", line)
              if m:
                  mapping[m.group(2).lower()] = m.group(1).lower()

          folder = mapping.get(author.lower())
          if not folder:
              print(f"::warning::No agent-logs folder mapped to @{author}. Skipping log-presence check.")
              sys.exit(0)

          diff = subprocess.check_output(
              ["git", "diff", "--name-only", base, head], text=True,
          ).splitlines()

          touched_source = [
              p for p in diff
              if not p.startswith("docs/")
              and not p.startswith(".github/")
              and not p.startswith(".claude/")
              and p
          ]
          new_logs = [p for p in diff if p.startswith(f"docs/agent-logs/{folder}/") and p.endswith(".jsonl")]

          if touched_source and not new_logs:
              print(f"::error::PR touches source files but does not include a new docs/agent-logs/{folder}/*.jsonl session log.")

content.text:
**Root cause**: gate checks `git diff base..head` for `.jsonl` files in your log folder. PR fails if source files changed but no `.jsonl` was added/modified IN THIS PR's diff.

**Why every time**:
- Claude Code session = 1 jsonl file named by session UUID (e.g. `2026-05-23_e6f6f51f-...jsonl`)
- File is `SessionEnd`-hook driven — flushes only when Claude Code session ends
- Mid-session, no commits touch the log → each new PR has "source changed but log untouched" → fail
- Each PR needs the log file flushed + committed before the gate accepts it

Fix this PR by re-flushing the log:

content.text:
Pushed `0e2bd69`. Log-presence should pass now.

---

## Long-term fix options

To stop this from happening every PR, three approaches:

**A. Relax the gate** — change `ci.yml` so the check passes if a `.jsonl` for today's date **exists** in the folder (regardless of whether this PR modified it). Reasonable since the spirit of the rule is "session was logged", not "log must change per PR".

**B. Pre-push hook** — `.husky/pre-push` or similar runs `scripts/copy-claude-session.sh` + amends the log into the latest commit. Automates the flush.

**C. Status quo + discipline** — manually run `scripts/copy-claude-session.sh` + commit before each `git push` of a PR-bound branch.

Recommend **A**: cheapest, removes 80% of the friction. Single ci.yml change. Want me to draft it?

message.content: go with option A

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  flutter-test:
    name: Flutter analyze + test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: app
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
        with:
          channel: 'stable'
          cache: true
      - run: flutter pub get
      - run: flutter analyze
      - run: flutter test --coverage
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: flutter-coverage
          path: app/coverage/lcov.info

  functions-test:
    name: Cloud Functions lint + test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: functions
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: functions/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm test -- --ci --coverage --testPathIgnorePatterns=__rules_tests__
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: functions-coverage
          path: functions/coverage

  rules-test:
    name: Firestore rules emulator test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: functions
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: functions/package-lock.json
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: '21'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm install -g firebase-tools
      - run: firebase emulators:exec --only firestore "npm run test -- --ci --testPathPattern __rules_tests__"

  secrets-scan:
    name: gitleaks secret scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

  log-presence:
    name: Agent log presence
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.13'
      - name: Check that PR author committed a session log
        env:
          PR_AUTHOR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        run: |
          python - <<'PY'
          import os, re, subprocess, sys
          author = os.environ.get("PR_AUTHOR", "")
          base = os.environ.get("BASE_SHA", "")
          head = os.environ.get("HEAD_SHA", "")

          # Resolve GitHub handle → folder name via docs/agent-logs/README.md table.
          mapping = {}
          for line in open("docs/agent-logs/README.md", encoding="utf-8"):
              m = re.match(r"\|\s*\w+\s*\|\s*`?(\w+)`?\s*\|\s*@?([\w-]+)\s*\|", line)
              if m:
                  mapping[m.group(2).lower()] = m.group(1).lower()

          folder = mapping.get(author.lower())
          if not folder:
              print(f"::warning::No agent-logs folder mapped to @{author}. Skipping log-presence check.")
              sys.exit(0)

          diff = subprocess.check_output(
              ["git", "diff", "--name-only", base, head], text=True,
          ).splitlines()

          touched_source = [
              p for p in diff
              if not p.startswith("docs/")
              and not p.startswith(".github/")
              and not p.startswith(".claude/")
              and p
          ]
          new_logs = [p for p in diff if p.startswith(f"docs/agent-logs/{folder}/") and p.endswith(".jsonl")]

          if touched_source and not new_logs:
              print(f"::error::PR touches source files but does not include a new docs/agent-logs/{folder}/*.jsonl session log.")
              print("Touched:")
              for p in touched_source:
                  print(f"  {p}")
              sys.exit(1)
          print(f"log-presence OK ({len(new_logs)} new log(s)).")
          PY

input.new_string:
- name: Check that PR author has a session log for the latest commit date
        env:
          PR_AUTHOR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        run: |
          python - <<'PY'
          import os, re, subprocess, sys
          from pathlib import Path

          author = os.environ.get("PR_AUTHOR", "")
          base = os.environ.get("BASE_SHA", "")
          head = os.environ.get("HEAD_SHA", "")

          # Resolve GitHub handle → folder name via docs/agent-logs/README.md table.
          mapping = {}
          for line in open("docs/agent-logs/README.md", encoding="utf-8"):
              m = re.match(r"\|\s*\w+\s*\|\s*`?(\w+)`?\s*\|\s*@?([\w-]+)\s*\|", line)
              if m:
                  mapping[m.group(2).lower()] = m.group(1).lower()

          folder = mapping.get(author.lower())
          if not folder:
              print(f"::warning::No agent-logs folder mapped to @{author}. Skipping log-presence check.")
              sys.exit(0)

          diff = subprocess.check_output(
              ["git", "diff", "--name-only", base, head], text=True,
          ).splitlines()
          touched_source = [
              p for p in diff
              if not p.startswith("docs/")
              and not p.startswith(".github/")
              and not p.startswith(".claude/")
              and p
          ]
          if not touched_source:
              print("log-presence OK (no source files touched).")
              sys.exit(0)

          # Relaxed rule: require a log file whose YYYY-MM-DD prefix matches the
          # latest commit's author date. A single log can satisfy multiple PRs
          # cut from the same day (e.g. small follow-ups), so we no longer
          # require the log to appear in this PR's diff.
          commit_date = subprocess.check_output(
              ["git", "show", "-s", "--format=%ad", "--date=format:%Y-%m-%d", head],
              text=True,
          ).strip()

          folder_path = Path(f"docs/agent-logs/{folder}")
          if not folder_path.is_dir():
              print(f"::error::No log folder at {folder_path} for @{author}.")
              sys.exit(1)

          today_logs = sorted(
              p.name for p in folder_path.glob("*.jsonl")
              if p.name.startswith(commit_date)
          )

          if not today_logs:
              print(f"::error::PR touches source files but @{author} has no docs/agent-logs/{folder}/{commit_date}_*.jsonl session log.")
              print(f"Latest commit ({head[:7]}) authored {commit_date}; expected at least one log file with that date prefix.")
              print("Touched:")
              for p in touched_source:
                  print(f"  {p}")
              print("To fix: run `scripts/copy-claude-session.sh` (or wait for the SessionEnd hook) and commit the resulting .jsonl on this branch.")
              sys.exit(1)

          print(f"log-presence OK ({len(today_logs)} log(s) for {commit_date}: {', '.join(today_logs)}).")
          PY

              ["git", "diff", "--name-only", base, head], text=True,
          ).splitlines()
          touched_source = [
              p for p in diff
              if not p.startswith("docs/")
              and not p.startswith(".github/")
              and not p.startswith(".claude/")
              and p
          ]
          if not touched_source:
              print("log-presence OK (no source files touched).")
              sys.exit(0)

          # Relaxed rule: require a log file whose YYYY-MM-DD prefix matches the
          # latest commit's author date. A single log can satisfy multiple PRs
          # cut from the same day (e.g. small follow-ups), so we no longer
          # require the log to appear in this PR's diff.
          commit_date = subprocess.check_output(
              ["git", "show", "-s", "--format=%ad", "--date=format:%Y-%m-%d", head],
              text=True,
          ).strip()

          folder_path = Path(f"docs/agent-logs/{folder}")
          if not folder_path.is_dir():
              print(f"::error::No log folder at {folder_path} for @{author}.")
              sys.exit(1)

          today_logs = sorted(
              p.name for p in folder_path.glob("*.jsonl")
              if p.name.startswith(commit_date)
          )

          if not today_logs:
              print(f"::error::PR touches source files but @{author} has no docs/agent-logs/{folder}/{commit_date}_*.jsonl session log.")
              print(f"Latest commit ({head[:7]}) authored {commit_date}; expected at least one log file with that date prefix.")
              print("Touched:")
              for p in touched_source:
                  print(f"  {p}")
              print("To fix: run scripts/copy-claude-session.sh (or wait for the SessionEnd hook) and commit the resulting .jsonl on this branch.")
              sys.exit(1)

          print(f"log-presence OK ({len(today_logs)} log(s) for {commit_date}: {', '.join(today_logs)}).")

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  flutter-test:
    name: Flutter analyze + test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: app
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
        with:
          channel: 'stable'
          cache: true
      - run: flutter pub get
      - run: flutter analyze
      - run: flutter test --coverage
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: flutter-coverage
          path: app/coverage/lcov.info

  functions-test:
    name: Cloud Functions lint + test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: functions
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: functions/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm test -- --ci --coverage --testPathIgnorePatterns=__rules_tests__
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: functions-coverage
          path: functions/coverage

  rules-test:
    name: Firestore rules emulator test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: functions
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: functions/package-lock.json
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: '21'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm install -g firebase-tools
      - run: firebase emulators:exec --only firestore "npm run test -- --ci --testPathPattern __rules_tests__"

  secrets-scan:
    name: gitleaks secret scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

  log-presence:
    name: Agent log presence
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.13'
      - name: Check that PR author committed a session log
        env:
          PR_AUTHOR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        run: |
          python - <<'PY'
          import os, re, subprocess, sys
          author = os.environ.get("PR_AUTHOR", "")
          base = os.environ.get("BASE_SHA", "")
          head = os.environ.get("HEAD_SHA", "")

          # Resolve GitHub handle → folder name via docs/agent-logs/README.md table.
          mapping = {}
          for line in open("docs/agent-logs/README.md", encoding="utf-8"):
              m = re.match(r"\|\s*\w+\s*\|\s*`?(\w+)`?\s*\|\s*@?([\w-]+)\s*\|", line)
              if m:
                  mapping[m.group(2).lower()] = m.group(1).lower()

          folder = mapping.get(author.lower())
          if not folder:
              print(f"::warning::No agent-logs folder mapped to @{author}. Skipping log-presence check.")
              sys.exit(0)

          diff = subprocess.check_output(
              ["git", "diff", "--name-only", base, head], text=True,
          ).splitlines()

          touched_source = [
              p for p in diff
              if not p.startswith("docs/")
              and not p.startswith(".github/")
              and not p.startswith(".claude/")
              and p
          ]
          new_logs = [p for p in diff if p.startswith(f"docs/agent-logs/{folder}/") and p.endswith(".jsonl")]

          if touched_source and not new_logs:
              print(f"::error::PR touches source files but does not include a new docs/agent-logs/{folder}/*.jsonl session log.")
              print("Touched:")
              for p in touched_source:
                  print(f"  {p}")
              sys.exit(1)
          print(f"log-presence OK ({len(new_logs)} new log(s)).")
          PY

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/ci.yml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
ci(log-presence): relax to require day's log, not per-PR diff change

The log-presence gate previously failed any PR that touched source
without including a *.jsonl change in the PR's diff. That's redundant
work — the log file's content is append-only across a single Claude
Code session, so multiple PRs cut from the same day were each forced
to re-flush and re-commit the same growing log. CI noise without value.

New rule: a *.jsonl file dated with the latest commit's author date
(YYYY-MM-DD prefix) must exist in the author's docs/agent-logs/<folder>/.
One log per day satisfies all PRs landed that day; the SessionEnd hook
fires once when the session ends and the gate is happy with that.

Side benefits:
- No `git diff` of the log file required, so squash-merges that drop
  the log commit during merge still pass on later PRs.
- The error message now tells the author exactly which date and folder
  the gate looked at, and recommends running scripts/copy-claude-session.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <[REDACTED:email]>
EOF
)" && git push

content.text:
Pushed `46a424f` to PR #8.

**New gate logic**:
- If no source files touched → pass immediately
- Else: look in `docs/agent-logs/<folder>/` for any `*.jsonl` whose filename starts with the latest commit's author date (e.g. `2026-05-23_*.jsonl`)
- If at least one exists → pass
- If none → fail with a helpful message naming the date + folder + recommended fix command

**Net effect**:
- One log flush per day satisfies every PR opened that day
- Small follow-up PRs no longer need a fresh log commit
- The log file's git history can still get squash-merged out without breaking subsequent PRs

After PR #8 merges, future PRs only need the log flushed once per day (typically at session end via the hook). The error message itself tells the author what to do if it ever fails.

content: CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE (full). Drop articles/filler/pleasantries/hedging. Fragments OK. Code/commits/security: write normal.

content.text:
Yes — best practices for a 5-dev, 12-day, agent-assisted class project:

## Practices applied to this issue set

| Practice | Application |
|---|---|
| **Single milestone** | "2026-06-04 submission" — all 22 issues attached |
| **Labels** | Triage (`ready-for-agent` default) + domain (`auth`/`request`/`tracking`/`fleet`/`widget`/`infra`) |
| **Assignee per RACI** | One owner per issue — `@AokDesu`/`@BBelleysp`/`@SNOWxSAUSAGES`/`@Mrmo0p`/`@Tantawan7` |
| **Body template** | Context + AC (Gherkin from `09-page-flow-design.md`) + Files-to-touch + Spec link + Test plan + Blockers |
| **Blockers in body** | `Blocked by #N` markdown for issues that depend on Belle's widget API stub |
| **AFK-ready phrasing** | "Definition of done" written so Claude Code can self-verify |
| **Link to prototype screen** | Each page issue references `docs/prototype-screens/<id>.png` + the spec §section |
| **No premature subdivision** | Sub-tasks live in the dev's `TodoWrite` session, not as child issues |

## Final 22-issue list (re-confirm before filing)

**Skipped**: P-U-01 Login, P-U-02 Register (auth flow done, merged in PR #3+#6).

**Pages — 14 issues**
| # | Owner | Title |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | P-U-03 Request — catalog browse + cart + submit |
| 2 | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | P-U-04 Queue — pending requests list |
| 3 | @Mrmo0p | P-U-05 Tracking — live map + battery + ETA |
| 4 | @Mrmo0p | P-U-06 Confirm — delivery confirm flow |
| 5 | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | P-U-07 History — past requests list |
| 6 | @Mrmo0p | P-U-08 Notifications — in-app inbox |
| 7 | @BBelleysp | P-U-09 Profile + Settings + logout dialog |
| 8 | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | P-A-01 Admin Requests list |
| 9 | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | P-A-02 Admin Request Manage (approve/reject + drone picker) |
| 10 | @Tantawan7 | P-A-03 Admin Drone list |
| 11 | @Tantawan7 | P-A-04 Admin Drone detail |
| 12 | @Mrmo0p | P-A-05 Admin Control live map |
| 13 | @Tantawan7 | P-A-06 Admin Weather panel |
| 14 | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | P-A-07 Admin Inventory + restock |

**Shared widgets — 4 issues (Belle, ADR-0003 enforces Day-4 API freeze)**
| # | Owner | Title |
|---|---|---|
| 15 | @BBelleysp | Widget API stubs (Day 4 freeze) — DroneMap, BatteryBar, StatusChip, ItemPicker signatures |
| 16 | @BBelleysp | DroneMap body — flutter_map + marker layer |
| 17 | @BBelleysp | BatteryBar body — animated progress |
| 18 | @BBelleysp | StatusChip + ItemPicker bodies |

**Infra / spike — 4 issues**
| # | Owner | Title |
|---|---|---|
| 19 | @AokDesu | functions/src/lib/fcm.ts — stale token cleanup |
| 20 | @Mrmo0p | flutter_map spike — throwaway POC, surface integration risks Day 4 |
| 21 | @AokDesu | Demo seed dataset polish — preset requests + flights for demo recording |
| 22 | @AokDesu | Demo screencast + README screenshots |

**Dropped from earlier list**:
- ~~Windows setup doc~~ — your team uses Claude Code which reads README itself; setup walks itself through. Skip unless you want explicit doc.
- ~~README "Known gaps" update~~ — already done in PR #7.

---

## Two execution questions before I file

**Q1.** Confirm the 22-issue list above OR drop/add specific items?

**Q2.** Filing mechanism:
- **A.** I run `gh issue create` 22 times in this session with the body template (full control, ~5 min)
- **B.** I invoke `to-issues` skill which auto-extracts AC from `09-page-flow-design.md` per page (may overshoot or undershoot — needs review)

Recommend **A** — more predictable for a high-leverage one-shot. Tell me confirmed and I file.

**Page**: P-U-03 (user/home)
**Owner**: @SNOWxSAUSAGES (Bew, per RACI)
**Spec**: [`docs/09-page-flow-design.md` §5 P-U-03](../docs/09-page-flow-design.md)

**Backend**: `submitRequest` callable already deployed in `functions/src/callable/submitRequest.ts` — validates payload + max weight 6kg + stock availability + fans out FCM to admins.

## Context
User's primary action: browse the catalog, build a cart of relief items, drop a pin on the map for delivery location, submit. On success → land on `/user/queue` (P-U-04).

## Acceptance criteria
- Browse the 6 seeded catalog items: name, icon, weightKg, stock count visible.
- Tap an item → add to cart with qty selector (1-10).
- Live total-weight indicator; submit disabled when total > 6kg.
- Pin picker on `flutter_map` (OpenStreetMap tiles) — default to user's `deliveryAddress` if set, else Bangkok center.
- Submit button → calls `submitRequest` callable with `{items:[{catalogId,qty}], deliveryAddress:{lat,lng,label}}`.
- On success: `go_router` to `/user/queue`.
- On failure: surface server error message inline (e.g. "Item out of stock", "Weight exceeds 6kg payload").

## Files to touch
- `app/lib/features/user/home_page.dart` (replace 18-line placeholder)
- `app/lib/features/user/request/` (new) — catalog repo, cart provider, pin-picker widget
- `app/test/features/user/home_page_test.dart` (new) — widget test for cart math + submit-disabled state

## Blockers
- Blocked by Belle's widget API stubs (Issue #15) for `ItemPicker` + map widget conventions.

## Test plan
- [ ] Widget test: cart total weight blocks submit at > 6kg
- [ ] Widget test: submit calls `submitRequest` with correct payload shape
- [ ] Manual on emulator: submit creates `requests/{reqId}` doc with status:pending
- [ ] `flutter analyze` clean
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Issue 2: P-U-04 Queue
gh issue create --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --assignee "SNOWxSAUSAGES" --label "ready-for-agent,request" --title "P-U-04 Queue — pending requests list" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**Page**: P-U-04 (user/queue)
**Owner**: @SNOWxSAUSAGES (Bew)
**Spec**: docs/09-page-flow-design.md §5 P-U-04
**Prototype**: docs/prototype-screens/user/P-U-04_queue.png

## Context
List of the signed-in user's requests with status pending or approved-not-yet-delivered. Live-updates via Firestore stream. Tap a row → P-U-05 Tracking (if a flight is assigned) OR detail/cancel UI.

## Acceptance criteria
- Stream `requests/` where `userId == auth.uid && status in ['pending','approved','assigned','in_flight']` (ordered by createdAt desc).
- Each row: item summary, total weight, status chip, createdAt relative time, drone ID if assigned.
- Empty state when no active requests.
- Pull-to-refresh.
- Tap on `status=in_flight` row → `/user/tracking/<flightId>`.
- Cancel button on `status=pending` rows → calls `cancelRequest` callable, confirm dialog first.

## Files to touch
- `app/lib/features/user/queue_page.dart` (new) — replace router placeholder
- `app/lib/features/user/request/queue_provider.dart` (new) — Riverpod stream

## Blockers
- Blocked by #15 (widget API stubs — `StatusChip`)

## Test plan
- [ ] Widget test: empty state + populated state render
- [ ] Manual: submit a request on P-U-03, see it appear here; cancel removes it
- [ ] `flutter analyze` clean
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Issue 3: P-U-05 Tracking
gh issue create --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --assignee "Mrmo0p" --label "ready-for-agent,tracking" --title "P-U-05 Tracking — live map + battery + ETA" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**Page**: P-U-05 (user/tracking/:flightId)
**Owner**: @Mrmo0p (Poom)
**Spec**: docs/09-page-flow-design.md §5 P-U-05
**Prototype**: docs/prototype-screens/user/P-U-05_tracking.png
**Backend**: `tickFlights` scheduled function publishes `flights/{id}` updates every 60s with progress, battery, eta. `sim.ts` math is ready.

## Context
Demo centerpiece. Stream a single flight doc; render the drone marker on `flutter_map`, smoothly interpolate position between 60-second server ticks, show battery + ETA + status chips.

## Acceptance criteria
- Stream `flights/{flightId}` (security rule allows owner read).
- Render OSM tiles + origin marker + destination marker + animated drone marker.
- Client-side interpolation between server ticks (~60s) so the drone visibly moves second-by-second.
- Overlay: BatteryBar (from shared widgets), ETA countdown, status chip.
- Aborted-flight state: replace drone with a warning marker + show abort reason.
- Delivered state: redirect to P-U-06 Confirm (`/user/confirm/<reqId>`).

## Files to touch
- `app/lib/features/user/tracking_page.dart` (new)
- `app/lib/features/user/tracking/interpolation.dart` (new) — pure math, unit-testable
- `app/lib/features/user/tracking/flight_provider.dart` (new)

## Blockers
- Blocked by #15 (widget API stubs — `DroneMap`, `BatteryBar`)
- Blocked by #20 (flutter_map spike) — confirms map approach works before this page commits

## Test plan
- [ ] Unit test: interpolation math returns smooth coords between ticks
- [ ] Manual on emulator: invoke `tickFlights` from Emulator UI → see drone move
- [ ] `flutter analyze` clean
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Issue 4: P-U-06 Confirm
gh issue create --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --assignee "Mrmo0p" --label "ready-for-agent,tracking" --title "P-U-06 Confirm — delivery confirm flow" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**Page**: P-U-06 (user/confirm/:reqId)
**Owner**: @Mrmo0p (Poom)
**Spec**: docs/09-page-flow-design.md §5 P-U-06
**Prototype**: docs/prototype-screens/user/P-U-06_confirm.png
**Backend**: `confirmDelivery` callable already deployed.

## Context
Reached automatically when flight reaches DELIVERING_HOLD (60s) and arrives. User confirms receipt → request marked completed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Show request summary + drone ID + delivered timestamp.
- "Confirm received" button → calls `confirmDelivery` with reqId.
- On success: redirect to `/user/history`.
- "Report problem" link → opens a free-text dialog, also calls `confirmDelivery` with `{reqId, complaint: '...'}`.

## Files to touch
- `app/lib/features/user/confirm_page.dart` (new)

## Test plan
- [ ] Manual: complete a delivery on emulator, confirm flow lands on history with status=completed
- [ ] `flutter analyze` clean
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Issue 6: P-U-08 Notifications
gh issue create --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --assignee "Mrmo0p" --label "ready-for-agent,tracking" --title "P-U-08 Notifications — in-app inbox + FCM device registration" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**Page**: P-U-08 (user/notifications)
**Owner**: @Mrmo0p (Poom)
**Spec**: docs/09-page-flow-design.md §5 P-U-08
**Prototype**: docs/prototype-screens/user/P-U-08_notifications.png
**Backend**: `users/{uid}/notifications/{nid}` subcollection populated by `onFlightWritten` trigger. Per ADR-0002, FCM push to device is exempt under emulator; inbox is the user-visible surface.

## Context
Combines two things: (a) the inbox list of notification docs written by Cloud Functions, and (b) FCM device-registration plumbing so tokens land on `users/{uid}.fcmTokens` (won't actually deliver under emulator, but the path must work for production).

## Acceptance criteria
- Stream `users/{uid}/notifications` desc by createdAt.
- Each row: title, body, deepLink, unread/read state.
- Tap row → mark `readAt: serverTimestamp()` (only field the security rule allows the client to patch) and follow deepLink.
- Empty state.
- FCM device registration on first app open (request permission, get token, write to `users/{uid}.fcmTokens` array via `updateProfile` callable extended for `fcmTokens` field — coordinate with Aok if updateProfile needs to accept fcmTokens).

## Files to touch
- `app/lib/features/user/notifications_page.dart` (new)
- `app/lib/features/user/notifications/fcm_register.dart` (new) — token retrieval + sync
- Possibly `functions/src/callable/updateProfile.ts` (coordinate with #19) to accept fcmTokens

## Blockers
- Blocked by #15 (widget API stubs) for chip styling.

## Test plan
- [ ] Widget test: inbox empty + populated
- [ ] Manual on emulator: submit a request, see admin notification appear in admin's inbox; tap marks readAt
- [ ] `flutter analyze` clean
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Issue 7: P-U-09 Profile

# Issue 9: P-A-02 Admin Request Manage
gh issue create --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --assignee "SNOWxSAUSAGES" --label "ready-for-agent,request" --title "P-A-02 Admin Request Manage (approve/reject + drone picker)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**Page**: P-A-02 (admin/requests/:reqId)
**Owner**: @SNOWxSAUSAGES (Bew)
**Spec**: docs/09-page-flow-design.md §6 P-A-02
**Prototype**: docs/prototype-screens/admin/P-A-02_request_manage.png
**Backend**: `approveRequest`, `rejectRequest`, `assignDrone` callables ready.

## Context
Detail view of a single pending request. Admin can approve (+ pick a drone) or reject (with reason). Approving a request without an assigned drone immediately is OK — drone picker fans out from approveRequest flow OR a separate assignDrone callable.

## Acceptance criteria
- Show full request payload, user info, delivery address (mini map).
- Approve flow: opens drone picker (idle drones with capacity >= total weight) → calls `approveRequest` then `assignDrone`.
- Reject flow: textarea for reason → calls `rejectRequest`.
- After action: navigate back to P-A-01 (the list now reflects the new status).

## Files to touch
- `app/lib/features/admin/request_manage_page.dart` (new)
- `app/lib/features/admin/requests/drone_picker.dart` (new)

## Blockers
- Blocked by #15 (widget API stubs)
- Coordinate drone availability stream with @Tantawan7's P-A-03 (#10) provider.

## Test plan
- [ ] Widget test: drone picker filters by capacity
- [ ] Manual on emulator: approve a request, drone assigned, flight starts
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

This PR unblocks Bew, Poom, Tawan. Four widget files with **frozen public APIs** but placeholder bodies (Container with right size). Consumers code against the API from Day 4; you fill in real renderers Days 5-7 (#16, #17, #18) without breaking them.

- `app/lib/core/widgets/battery_bar.dart` — `{required double percent, double? height}`. Body: `LinearProgressIndicator(value: percent / 100)` placeholder is acceptable.
- `app/lib/core/widgets/status_chip.dart` — `{required String status, EdgeInsets? padding}`. Body: simple `Chip(label: Text(status))` placeholder.
- `app/lib/core/widgets/item_picker.dart` — `{required List<CatalogItem> items, required ValueChanged<List<CartLine>> onChanged}`. Body: `Text('ItemPicker placeholder')` is acceptable.
- Each widget exported via `app/lib/core/widgets.dart` barrel file.

## Files to touch

- `app/lib/core/widgets/battery_bar.dart` (new)
- `app/lib/core/widgets/status_chip.dart` (new)

- `app/lib/core/widgets.dart` (new) — barrel

**Owner**: @BBelleysp (Belle)
**Spec**: docs/09-page-flow-design.md §3 shared widgets

## Context
Final two widget bodies. StatusChip uses themed colors per status; ItemPicker uses Bew's catalog repo (P-U-03).

## Acceptance criteria
- StatusChip: color map per status string (pending=blue, approved=green, rejected=red, in_flight=orange, completed=grey, cancelled=grey, aborted=red).
- ItemPicker: list of catalog items with stepper buttons; emits `List<CartLine>` to `onChanged`.

# Issue 19: FCM stale token cleanup
gh issue create --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --assignee "AokDesu" --label "ready-for-agent,infra" --title "functions/src/lib/fcm.ts — stale token cleanup" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**Owner**: @AokDesu
**Spec**: §11 in master design spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-drone-relief-design.md)
**Existing TODO**: \`functions/src/lib/fcm.ts:4\`

## Context
FCM fan-out currently sends to every token on a user/admin doc. If a token is invalid (uninstalled app, expired), it stays forever. Need to inspect the SendEachForMulticast response, identify failed token indices, and remove them from `users/{uid}.fcmTokens`.

## Acceptance criteria
- After `sendEachForMulticast`, inspect `res.responses[].error.code`.
- For codes `messaging/registration-token-not-registered` and `messaging/invalid-registration-token`, collect the corresponding tokens from the input array.
- Patch `users/{uid}.fcmTokens = arrayRemove(...staleTokens)`.
- Add a unit test in `functions/__tests__/fcm.test.ts` (mock messaging response).

## Files to touch
- `functions/src/lib/fcm.ts`
- `functions/__tests__/fcm.test.ts` (new)

## Test plan
- [ ] Unit test: 3 tokens, 1 stale → patch removes only the stale one
- [ ] `npm run lint` + `npm test` clean
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

# Issue 20: flutter_map spike
gh issue create --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --assignee "Mrmo0p" --label "ready-for-agent,tracking" --title "flutter_map spike — throwaway POC, Day 4" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
**Owner**: @Mrmo0p (Poom)
**Deadline**: Day 4 (2026-05-25) — surface integration risks before P-U-05 + P-A-05 commit.
**Skill**: use the \`prototype\` skill if available.

## Context
Pre-flight check for the tracking page. Build a throwaway scaffold that proves: OSM tiles load on Android emulator, `flutter_map` plays nice with go_router + Riverpod versions, custom Marker rendering works, smooth animation between marker positions is achievable.

## Acceptance criteria
- Branch `spike/flutter-map` (do NOT merge to main).
- Single page renders a flutter_map with OSM tiles + a moving marker that animates between two waypoints every 2s.
- Report (as a PR comment or new issue): what worked, what didn't, version pins required, any gotchas (CORS, tile-server rate limits, OverlayPane layering, etc.).
- If a blocker is found, escalate to @AokDesu + adjust P-U-05 (#11) approach.

## Files to touch
- `spike/flutter-map` branch, throwaway scaffold inside `app/`.

## Test plan
- [ ] Map renders + marker moves smoothly on the Android emulator
- [ ] Report posted as a comment on #11

## Outcome
- If spike succeeds → P-U-05 and P-A-05 proceed as planned
- If spike fails → adjust scope OR fallback to a static map
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

content.text:
Yes. After collaborator invites + acceptances, each dev runs in their Claude Code session:

```bash
gh issue list --assignee @me --state open --milestone "2026-06-04 submission"
```

Or by explicit handle (works regardless of who you're authed as):

```bash
gh issue list --assignee BBelleysp --state open
```

## Per-dev workflow (each team member, in their own Claude Code session)

1. **Authenticate gh once** as themselves:
   ```bash
   gh auth login
   # Pick HTTPS + login with web browser + accept the invite first
   ```

2. **List own issues**:
   ```bash
   gh issue list --assignee @me --milestone "2026-06-04 submission"
   ```

3. **Pick an issue + ask Claude Code to work it**:
   > "Implement issue #16 — admin requests list"
   
   Claude reads the issue body (which contains AC + files + spec links + blockers), checks blockers status, creates a branch like `feat/p-a-01-admin-requests`, implements per AC, runs `flutter analyze`, opens PR with `Closes #16` in the body so GitHub auto-closes the issue on merge.

4. **Track progress visually**:
   - GitHub Projects board (optional but nice) — Settings → Projects → New project → "Board" view, group by Status (Todo / In progress / In review / Done)
   - Or just `gh issue list --label "ready-for-agent" --milestone "2026-06-04 submission" --state open`

## Useful one-liners for the team to drop in their Claude Code prompts

| Goal | Command |
|---|---|
| My open issues | `gh issue list --assignee @me --state open` |
| Unblocked widgets ready to start | `gh issue list --label widget --state open` |
| All blockers I depend on | (grep PR body for `Blocked by #N` per issue, or just open the issue) |
| Comment progress | `gh issue comment <N> --body "..."` |
| Move out of "ready-for-agent" once you start | `gh issue edit <N> --remove-label ready-for-agent --add-label in-progress` |

(Add `in-progress` label first if you want that state — currently we only have triage labels.)

---

- **B.** Add an `in-progress` label so devs can mark active work, or
- **C.** Set up a GitHub Projects board with To-do / In progress / In review / Done columns auto-linked to issues by label?

Or all three?

file.content:
# Issue tracker: GitHub

Issues and PRDs for this repo live as GitHub issues. Use the `gh` CLI for all operations.

## Conventions

- **Create an issue**: `gh issue create --title "..." --body "..."`. Use a heredoc for multi-line bodies.
- **Read an issue**: `gh issue view <number> --comments`, filtering comments by `jq` and also fetching labels.
- **List issues**: `gh issue list --state open --json number,title,body,labels,comments --jq '[.[] | {number, title, body, labels: [.labels[].name], comments: [.comments[].body]}]'` with appropriate `--label` and `--state` filters.
- **Comment on an issue**: `gh issue comment <number> --body "..."`
- **Apply / remove labels**: `gh issue edit <number> --add-label "..."` / `--remove-label "..."`
- **Close**: `gh issue close <number> --comment "..."`

Infer the repo from `git remote -v` — `gh` does this automatically when run inside a clone.

## When a skill says "publish to the issue tracker"

Create a GitHub issue.

## When a skill says "fetch the relevant ticket"

Run `gh issue view <number> --comments`.

content.text: Yes — three small doc updates worth bundling before break. CLAUDE.md is thin, `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md` doesn't describe the per-dev workflow yet, and a few stale claims linger.

## When a skill says "fetch the relevant ticket"

Run `gh issue view <number> --comments`.
input.new_string:
## When a skill says "fetch the relevant ticket"

Run `gh issue view <number> --comments`.

## Working an issue (per-dev workflow)

For team members picking up an issue from the "2026-06-04 submission" milestone (issues #9-#30 as of 2026-05-23):

```bash
# 1. Authenticate once per machine (each dev as themselves):
gh auth login

# 2. List your assigned, open issues:
gh issue list --assignee @me --state open --milestone "2026-06-04 submission"

# 3. Pick one and read it:
gh issue view <number> --comments

# 4. Branch + implement (typical branch name: feat/<page-id>-<short>):
git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/p-a-01-admin-requests

# 5. Open the PR with auto-close link to the issue:
gh pr create --base main --title "..." --body "Closes #<number>\n\n..."
```

`Closes #N` (or `Fixes #N` / `Resolves #N`) in the PR body auto-closes the issue when the PR merges.

## Blockers

Several issues have `Blocked by #N` in their body (e.g. consumer pages blocked by Belle's widget API stubs #23 per ADR-0003). Don't start a blocked issue until the dependency PR is merged. Use `gh issue view <N>` to check current status of the blocker.

## Repo membership

GitHub assignment only sticks for users with at least read access on the repo. Lead invites teammates as collaborators via `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/collaborators/<handle> -X PUT -f permission=push` (or the Settings → Manage access UI). Issue body still says "Owner: @handle" even before the invite is accepted, so the intent is preserved.

+For team members picking up an issue from the "2026-06-04 submission" milestone (issues #9-#30 as of 2026-05-23):
+# 1. Authenticate once per machine (each dev as themselves):
+# 5. Open the PR with auto-close link to the issue:
+`Closes #N` (or `Fixes #N` / `Resolves #N`) in the PR body auto-closes the issue when the PR merges.

+Several issues have `Blocked by #N` in their body (e.g. consumer pages blocked by Belle's widget API stubs #23 per ADR-0003). Don't start a blocked issue until the dependency PR is merged. Use `gh issue view <N>` to check current status of the blocker.

+GitHub assignment only sticks for users with at least read access on the repo. Lead invites teammates as collaborators via `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/collaborators/<handle> -X PUT -f permission=push` (or the Settings → Manage access UI). Issue body still says "Owner: @handle" even before the invite is accepted, so the intent is preserved.

# CSC291-DroneAid

> Class project on the Firebase Emulator Suite. Deadline 2026-06-04; submission 2026-06-05. No real Firebase deploy (see `docs/adr/0002-scope-full-features-fcm-emulator-exempt.md`).

## Running the stack

Single command for the full stack (emulators + seed on first run + Flutter app):

```bash
bun scripts/dev.ts
```

State persists in gitignored `./.emulator-data/` between runs. See README "Daily run loop" for prerequisites + the wipe-and-reseed escape hatch.

## Working an issue

22 issues attached to milestone **"2026-06-04 submission"** (#9-#30). Standard per-dev flow:

```bash
gh issue list --assignee @me --state open --milestone "2026-06-04 submission"
gh issue view <number> --comments
git checkout -b feat/<short>
# ...implement per the issue body's AC...
gh pr create --base main --body "Closes #<number>\n\n..."
```

Issue bodies contain: spec link, acceptance criteria, files to touch, blockers (`Blocked by #N`), test plan. Honour blockers — many consumer pages are gated on Belle's widget API stubs (#23) per `docs/adr/0003-widget-api-stub-first.md`.

## Architecture decisions

`docs/adr/` records hard-to-reverse decisions:

- **0001** — Native Windows is the dev-env standard for non-lead devs.
- **0002** — "Done" means full features on the emulator; FCM-push-to-device is exempt.
- **0003** — Belle ships widget API stubs Day 4 (frozen public API) so consumers can start in parallel.

## Agent skills

### Issue tracker

Issues live in GitHub Issues at `AokDesu/CSC291-DroneAid`, accessed via the `gh` CLI. See `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md`.

### Triage labels

Canonical vocabulary (created in the repo): `needs-triage`, `needs-info`, `ready-for-agent`, `ready-for-human`, `wontfix`. Domain labels: `auth`, `request`, `tracking`, `fleet`, `widget`, `infra`, `demo`. See `docs/agents/triage-labels.md`.

### Domain docs

Single-context layout: `CONTEXT.md` (not created yet — `/grill-with-docs` creates it lazily when a domain term is first disambiguated) + `docs/adr/` at repo root. See `docs/agents/domain.md`.

## Domain labels (also created in the repo)

Layered alongside the triage labels for filtering:

| Label | Use for |
|---|---|
| `auth` | Identity / login / register / role guard |
| `request` | Request domain (catalog, queue, history, admin requests, inventory) |
| `tracking` | Tracking, maps, FCM, notifications, control map |
| `fleet` | Drone fleet (list, detail, weather) |
| `widget` | Shared UI widgets |
| `infra` | Backend, build, CI, scripts, ops |
| `demo` | Demo prep (seed dataset, screenshots, screencast) |

Useful filter combos:

```bash
gh issue list --label "ready-for-agent,widget"     # widgets ready to start
gh issue list --label "ready-for-agent,tracking"   # tracking work
gh issue list --label "ready-for-human"            # needs human (e.g. demo screencast)
```
